structural_analysis.missing_signal_hypothesis: The agent receives dense shaping rewards for being in the approach region, leading to hovering exploitation without ever completing a stable landing. A sparse milestone reward for entering the landing region is missing to guide progress toward the terminal goal.
structural_analysis.why_local_edit_is_insufficient: Existing local weight adjustments have not broken the hovering cycle; a new sparse milestone is necessary to bridge the gap between approach region and terminal success.
safety_constraints: Event bonus is one-time and gated by duration to prevent exploitation.

reward_editor.rationale: Adding a one-time event bonus for sustained presence in the landing region provides a safe, non-exploitable intermediate reward. It uses only configured events and respects the one-time and duration constraints to avoid turning into a repeatable exploit.

diagnosis: Shaping-goal mismatch and repeated event exploitation due to dominating approach region reward; adding a sparse landing region milestone.
